---
title: ""
page-layout: custom
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .navbar::before {
    background: transparent !important;
  }
</style>

<!-- Three.js Library -->
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/three.js/r134/three.min.js"></script>

<!-- WebGL Background Canvas -->
<canvas id="bg-canvas" style="position: absolute; top: -10px; left: 0; z-index: -1; width: 100vw; height: calc(100vh + 10px); mask-image: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(0,0,0,1) 0%, rgba(0,0,0,1) 40%, rgba(0,0,0,0) 90%); -webkit-mask-image: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(0,0,0,1) 0%, rgba(0,0,0,1) 40%, rgba(0,0,0,0) 90%); pointer-events: none;"></canvas>

<div class="container" style="padding: 0 2rem 4rem 2rem; max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto;">

  <div class="subject-hero">
    <h1 class="hero-title" style="color: #fff;">Information Theory for Machine Learning</h1>
    <p class="hero-subtitle">Quantifying uncertainty, information, and the distance between distributions.</p>
    <p class="hero-disclaimer" style="color: rgba(255,255,255,0.7); font-style: italic; max-width: 600px; margin: 1rem auto 0 auto;">Note: This section focuses exclusively on concepts relevant to Machine Learning and barely scratches the deep canopy of pure information theory.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="subject-intro">
    <p>
      <strong>Information theory</strong> provides the mathematical foundation for measuring,
      compressing, and transmitting data. At its core, it quantifies uncertainty and the amount of
      information carried by random events. The key insight is that observing a less probable event
      yields more information.
    </p>
    <p>
      In practical applications, these concepts are fundamental to modern machine learning and
      statistics. <em>Entropy</em> establishes the theoretical limits of data compression algorithms 
      used in everyday file zipping and video streaming. When we build models, 
      <em>cross-entropy</em> and the <em>Kullback&ndash;Leibler divergence</em> serve as the primary
      loss functions for classification tasks, enabling neural networks to learn the difference 
      between predicted probabilities and true labels.
    </p>
    <p>
      Advanced measures like <em>f-divergences</em> and the <em>Wasserstein distance</em> offer 
      powerful tools for comparing probability distributions, driving the optimization of Generative 
      Adversarial Networks (GANs). In Bayesian machine learning, the <em>Evidence Lower Bound (ELBO)</em> 
      makes Variational Inference computationally tractable, allowing us to approximate complex 
      posterior distributions and train Variational Autoencoders (VAEs). Together, these principles 
      form the rigorous backbone of modern probabilistic modeling and inference.
    </p>
  </div>

  <hr style="border: 0; border-top: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3); margin: 4rem 0;">

  <div class="section-title" style="margin-top: 4rem; margin-bottom: 3rem; text-align: center;">
    <h2 style="font-size: 2.5rem; margin-bottom: 1rem; color: #fff;">Core Information Measures</h2>
  </div>

  <div class="glass-grid">

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Information Content</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on information content.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/information-content.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Entropy</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on entropy.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/entropy.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Mutual Information</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on mutual information.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/mutual-information.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Cross-Entropy</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on cross-entropy.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/cross-entropy.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Perplexity</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on perplexity.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/perplexity.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="section-title" style="margin-top: 4rem; margin-bottom: 3rem; text-align: center;">
    <h2 style="font-size: 2.5rem; margin-bottom: 1rem; color: #fff;">Divergences and Distances</h2>
  </div>

  <div class="glass-grid">

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>KL Divergence</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on kl divergence.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/kl-divergence.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Jensen-Shannon Divergence</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on jensen-shannon divergence.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/jensen-shannon-divergence.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>f-Divergence</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on f-divergence.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/f-divergence.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Wasserstein Distance</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on wasserstein distance.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/wasserstein-distance.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="section-title" style="margin-top: 4rem; margin-bottom: 3rem; text-align: center;">
    <h2 style="font-size: 2.5rem; margin-bottom: 1rem; color: #fff;">Statistical Information</h2>
  </div>

  <div class="glass-grid">

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Fisher Information</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on fisher information.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/fisher-information.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="section-title" style="margin-top: 4rem; margin-bottom: 3rem; text-align: center;">
    <h2 style="font-size: 2.5rem; margin-bottom: 1rem; color: #fff;">Variational Objectives</h2>
  </div>

  <div class="glass-grid">

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Evidence Lower Bound (ELBO)</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on evidence lower bound (elbo).</p>
      <a href="information-theory/evidence-lower-bound-elbo.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="section-title" style="margin-top: 4rem; margin-bottom: 3rem; text-align: center;">
    <h2 style="font-size: 2.5rem; margin-bottom: 1rem; color: #fff;">Markov Information Processes</h2>
  </div>

  <div class="glass-grid">

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Markov Chain</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on markov chain.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/markov-chain.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Stochastic Matrices and Markov Chains</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on stochastic matrices and markov chains.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/stochastic-matrices-and-markov-chains.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="section-title" style="margin-top: 4rem; margin-bottom: 3rem; text-align: center;">
    <h2 style="font-size: 2.5rem; margin-bottom: 1rem; color: #fff;">Information-Theoretic Learning</h2>
  </div>

  <div class="glass-grid">

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Information Bottleneck</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on information bottleneck.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/information-bottleneck.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>

    <div class="glass-card">
      <h3>Evaluation Metrics for Ranking and Information Retrieval</h3>
      <p>Explore detailed notes on evaluation metrics for ranking and information retrieval.</p>
      <a href="information-theory/evaluation-metrics-for-ranking-and-information-retrieval.html" class="card-btn">Explore Notes</a>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
<script src="/assets/bg-digital-rain.js"></script>
<script src="/assets/card-tilt.js"></script>
```
